snippets.raw_ocr: MAXWELL GIRL SENTENCE IS DEFENDED WISE Va Nov 29 1N5 111feeling against what they de scribed as outsiders attempting to Interfere with their Jurisprudence was spreading among the elder hill folk today Hostility against the protests made by persons In other States against the 25year imprisonment imposed upon Edith Maxwell 21 for killing her father Trigg Max well was so pronounced that Edith was not allowed to see her mother yesterday Jim Dotson the jailer is Ediths uncle He said he refused the mother permission to visit Edith because I dont want to take any chances of increasing the bad feel ing among some of the folks here Because of this resentment among a certain class of her own people Edith had to eat her Thanksgiving dinner alone Her mother sent her a roast chicken vegetables and an angel food cake The mother faces trial next spring on a charge of being an accessory in the murder Meanwhile womens clubs in Virginia and in Tennessee ap pointed committees to come here and investigate Ediths case and seek to have the Jurys manda tory sentence of 25 years set aside either by a new trial or a Governors pardon Arguments for

snippets.raw_ocr: U SLOAN CURB FACES FIGHT ON HILL By FRANKLIN G SARTWELL Congressional opposition which Is rapidly developing to the ef forts of the Home Owners Loan Corporation to go out of business is giving the Administration a sorry foretaste of the difficulties it will encounter when and if it reaches the point of liquidating the many recently created emer gency organizations Although the corporation an nounced about a month ago that it has spent all its moneythat is that it had loaned it and that it believed it had covered the field for which it was created and therefore that it should discon tinue its loaning operations it was Indicated here today that it will not be permitted to be the first of the New Deal agencies to go out of business More Loans Sought Determined se n tim ent fyas sprung up in congressional circles to keep the corporation going and going as a lending agency reach ing into hundreds of thousands of homes This sentiment probably will take the form of bills which would appropriate at least an other thousand million dollars for the corporation to lend to citizens desiring to preserve their equities in their homes The
